title: They Spent $20 Million on Psychics. Then Said It Didn't Work.
title_es: Gastaron 20 mdd en psíquicos; luego dijeron que no funcionó
description: For 23 years the CIA ran a psychic intelligence program called Stargate. The official verdict: it didn't work. The declassified data tells a different story — 450+ missions, a Legion of Merit, a 89.5% agency return rate, and a closure decision that came three months before the evaluation.
description_es: Durante 23 años la CIA operó un programa de inteligencia psíquica llamado Stargate. Veredicto oficial: no funcionó. Los archivos desclasificados cuentan otra historia — más de 450 misiones, una Legión de Mérito, tasa de retorno del 89.5% y un cierre decidido tres meses antes de la evaluación.
author: Alejandro del Palacio
targetQuery: was the CIA Stargate program real
imageAlt: Heavily redacted CIA document on a steel desk under a single fluorescent lamp, black censor bars dominating the page
imagePrompt: Retrofuturist 1970s cinematic still photograph. Pitch-black government archive room dominates 70 percent of the frame in matte black and deep desaturated khaki with heavy 35mm grain. Center: a CIA document lying flat on a steel desk, the page heavily REDACTED with thick black censor bars covering most lines. Visible header reads STAR GATE PROJECT · DCI/SCIENCE & TECH · TOP SECRET in faded mimeograph blue. A red declassification stamp APPROVED FOR RELEASE 1995 angles across the top. Three small unredacted phrases peek through: "remote viewing," "target coordinates," "statistical effect p<.05." A vintage brass desk lamp throws a sharp circle of amber light onto the document; rest of the desk is in deep shadow. Worn government file folder, half-visible Polaroids of geographic targets, and a black rotary phone in soft focus background. The document + censor bars + amber lamplight + red stamp are the ONLY chromatic zone — cream paper, jet black bars, blueprint blue header, blood-red stamp, amber lamplight breaking out of the matte-black archive. Heavy 35mm film grain, anamorphic lens flare, kodak ektachrome retro slide aesthetic. Mood: All The President's Men + The Falcon and the Snowman + early X-Files. No recognizable face. Mysterious classified-archive forensic. Photographic NOT painterly NOT illustrated.
seoTitle: CIA Stargate Project: $20M, 24 Years, Declassified Documents
seoTitle_es: Proyecto Stargate CIA: $20M, 24 años, documentos desclasificados
seoDescription: The CIA's Stargate program ran 1972-1995 with $20M and 22 trained psychics. The declassified documents show what they actually found — and why it was buried.
seoDescription_es: El programa Stargate de la CIA operó 1972-1995 con $20M y 22 psíquicos entrenados. Los documentos desclasificados muestran lo que realmente encontraron — y por qué se enterró.
bluf: Yes — the CIA Stargate Program ran for 23 years (1972-1995) and produced 450+ tasked missions. The declassified record shows a 89.5% agency return rate, a Legion of Merit award to a remote viewer, and a closure decision made three months before the evaluation that justified it. The official it didnt work narrative is contradicted by the agencys own paperwork.
bluf_es: Sí — el Programa Stargate de la CIA operó durante 23 años (1972-1995) y produjo más de 450 misiones encargadas. El registro desclasificado muestra una tasa de retorno del 89.5%, una Legión al Mérito otorgada a un viewer remoto, y una decisión de cierre tomada tres meses antes de la evaluación que la justificó. La narrativa oficial de no funcionó es contradicha por los propios documentos de la agencia.
